response:
"Let us examine the data presented on the board with great care.

midnight approaches as we analyze the ratio of the largest values to the total sum.

The five greatest entries account for nearly one third of the entire collection.

The five smallest entries make up slightly less than one quarter of the whole.

Adding these two groups together leaves a significant portion of numbers unaccounted for.

We must determine if any overlap exists between the largest and smallest sets.

Since all values are positive, the remaining numbers must fill the gap between the two known sums.

A simple calculation reveals that exactly nine numbers satisfy all the given conditions.

This solution proves that the count of items written on the board is indeed nine."
